page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8i.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.g8i server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration, running on a KVM hypervisor.

This instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance appropriate for balanced workloads such as web serving, application hosting, microservices, and development environments that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8i.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance from the ecs.g8i family, built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via a KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile provides a stable, non-burstable resource allocation suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and microservices.
